data.title: AscendEX will list Raydium (RAY)
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: AscendEX (formerly BitMax) will launch Raydium (RAY) at 18:00 on March 20, opening the RAY/USDT trading pair, and deposits are now open.

data.title: JPMorgan CEO Highlights Economic Uncertainty Amid Global Tensions
data.description: According to Odaily, JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon stated on Wednesday at Adobe's annual summit in Las Vegas that the U.S. economy is still experiencing a 'soft landing,' but there are numerous external disruptions. Dimon noted that tariff policies have both advantages and disadvantages, causing anxiety in global markets. During a conversation with Adobe CEO Shantanu Narayen, he also emphasized that the tax reform plan being considered by U.S. President Donald Trump's administration adds to economic uncertainty.

data.title: Bybit CEO: 88.87% of stolen funds can still be traced, and mixer transactions have become the biggest challenge currently faced
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Bybit co-founder Ben Zhou released the latest progress on the stolen funds: 
Of the total $1.4 billion (about 500,000 ETH) of stolen funds, 88.87% can still be tracked, 7.59% have lost their traces, and 3.54% have been frozen. The report shows that 86.29% of the stolen funds (about 440,000 ETH, worth $1.23 billion) have been converted into 12,836 BTC, distributed in 9,117 wallets, with an average of 1.41 BTC per wallet. Hackers began to use BTC mixers such as Wasabi, CryptoMixer, Railgun and TornadoCash to transfer funds, of which 193 BTC mainly flowed to Wasabi mixers and were subsequently dispersed to multiple P2P traders. 
Ben Zhou said that decoding mixer transactions has become the biggest challenge currently faced, and called on more bounty hunters to participate in tracking and decoding. In the past 30 days, the platform received 5,012 bounty reports, of which 63 were valid reports.

data.title: JPMorgan Chase CEO: US economy is still in the "soft landing" stage
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon said at the Adobe Annual Summit in Las Vegas on Wednesday that the US economy is still in a "soft landing" phase, but "there are many external turbulent factors." "Tariff policies have both advantages and disadvantages, and the global market is anxious because of this." Dimon pointed out in a dialogue with Adobe CEO Shantanu Narayen. He also emphasized that the prospects of the tax reform plan being brewed by the Trump administration are unclear, exacerbating economic uncertainty. (Jinshi)

data.title: Bank of England Expected to Maintain Interest Rates Amid Economic Challenges
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, the Bank of England is anticipated to keep interest rates steady this week, aligning with the U.S. Federal Reserve's recent actions. However, it may implement more significant rate cuts later this year compared to the Federal Reserve. Similar to the U.S. central bank, the Bank of England expects inflation to continue easing in the coming months but aims to ensure that inflation reduction is on track and to avoid lowering borrowing costs too rapidly.

The British economy has been notably weaker than that of the United States. After stagnating in the third quarter, the UK economy grew by only 0.1% in the fourth quarter, narrowly avoiding a recession. Concerns over the impact of the Russia-Ukraine conflict and tariffs imposed by U.S. President Donald Trump on global economic growth have affected the UK, which also faces its own economic challenges. The government, led by Prime Minister Keir Starmer, has been striving to restore consumer and business confidence in the economic outlook.

The Bank of England has stated it is adopting a "gradual and cautious approach" to lowering interest rates. However, with the economy stagnating, the direction of interest rates is clear. Economists at Nomura Securities, led by George Buckley, predict that the central bank will maintain rates in March and cut them again in May. They foresee rate cuts at every meeting from now until February 2026, with a terminal rate of 3.5%.
